title: Bu Videodan Sonra Yeni Bir iPhone Almayacaksın !
transcript: I have collected all phones from iPhone 11 Pro Max to 17 Pro .  I will put exactly 7 iPhones through different tests, have them analyzed by artificial intelligence, and ask them to score each analysis.  At the end of the video we'll find out if we really need a new iPhone. Yaspe tries it.  The first stage is photography.  I take daytime shots, portrait shots, detail shots, video shots and night shots with all models. This step will take me a bit of time.  Because I have to give the AI ​​a shot so I don't confuse which iPhone I'm using . After sending them all to chat GPT, I want it to analyze the images in all shooting modes in detail and create a score table accordingly.  This is our first scoreboard. If we calculate it as a percentage, there is a 12% increase in just the transition from 11 to 12 .  Every model that came after it just seemed to add another spoonful of salt to the soup .  Yes, there is a clear difference between 11 and 17.  However, if we look at the price-performance balance here, the 13 Pro Max still makes more sense in terms of camera .  No, my concern is quality. If you say you are shooting cinematically, there is still no obvious difference between 15 and 17.  Next up is the performance test. In this, I found an application called Game Bench .  After running the application or game we want here, the program shows us how efficiently the FPS, graphics card, processor, memory and temperature values ​​​​are working.  I play PUBG Mobile, which I think will put the most pressure on the phone, for 15 minutes with all models and have the results analyzed one by one by the artificial intelligence.  This is our second scoreboard .  This is where things get a little weird.  While the highest FPS values ​​are at 13 and 17, 12 is more difficult than 11 on the processor side.  So in summary, the most stable device is the iPhone 17 Pro Max, the most efficient processor is the iPhone 14 Pro Max, and the highest thermal efficiency is the 16 Pro Max. In terms of performance, the iPhone 17 Pro Max is again mentioned.  The biggest leap in generation jumping is the transition from 12 to 13 Pro Max .  Again, if we consider the price-performance ratio, the 13 Pro Max still seems more reasonable.  Our next test is charging time and battery endurance times.  I find the results of the labs that have already done these tests on the internet and send them to the artificial intelligence .  This is how our scores are. Our next test is the component technologies inside the phones .  I need to have these analyzed individually.  Because, for example, there is a 5G technology that we will be transitioning to very soon .  While the iPhone 17 Pro supports 5G, the 11 Pro Max does not.  Likewise, Wi-Fi technologies are on this list with their weight and many features.  I have this information analyzed by artificial intelligence and create a scoreboard.  All well and good, but it is necessary to use them for a long time, to experience them and even to feel them.  That's why I'm collecting global data from a site that rates processors, graphics cards, micro- electromechanical systems, and user experiences This is the resulting scoreboard.  Now we come to the final scene.  I want him to compare all our test results and give us an overall scoreboard. This is the general picture.  In fact, not every new iPhone is faster.  Older iPhones are getting a little slower with the new iOS. This is planned obsolescence, the law of marketing. Every year, Apple gives by the teaspoon and takes by the scoop .  If you ask me, if you want the best but not the most expensive, go for the 15 or 16 Pro Max.  If you want a budget-friendly flagship, the 13 Pro Max.  If you say, “I’ll buy it now and not change it for another 5-6 years,” then it’s more logical to wear out at least one or two models to lower the price of the 17 Pro or Pro Max.  I've experienced it all.  You just choose one.

title: She found an iPhone 17 Pro Max _ Thought it’s fake, but it was real
transcript: Today this sister saw the iPhone 17 Pro Max on the pit. Seeing this she could not control herself and started trying to take it out. transcript: The iPhone 17 series will reportedly be available at the end of November or early November.  I'm sure some of you still can't decide whether to take the Pro or the Pro Max. It will be taken everywhere, taken traveling, put in a pocket, even accompany us to sleep, for example.  Don't choose the pro and then regret it later because you didn't choose the Pro Max or vice versa, you choose the Pro Max and it's too big in your hand.  Even though the plan is to use it for the next 3, 4, or even 5 years, for example.  I also plan to upgrade to the latest iPhone this year and I'm also considering whether to stay with the Pro Max this year or switch to the 17 Pro.  That's why I made this video, hopefully it can give you an idea of ​​which iPhone is best for your needs.  Is it 17 Pro or Pro Max. Currently, the only difference between the two is the screen size, weight of the iPhone, and battery life.  So, whatever you choose, there are no reduced features.  Personally, this year I'm more interested in the iPhone 17 Pro than the Pro Max.  And I have a few important reasons why I'm most likely switching to the Pro variant this year.  Even though previously I always used the Pro Max variant.  First of all, the size. Previously, the Pro variant was very small and could be said to be too small.   The iPhone 11 Pro is 5.8 inches and the Pro Max is 6.5 inches.  the size of an iPhone now.  Then it became 6.1 and 6.7 inches on the iPhone 12 Pro to the 15 Pro Series.  And on the iPhone 16 Pro and 17 Pro Series the size has increased by 2 inches, to 6.3 and 6.9 inches.  In my opinion, the 6.3 inch size fits perfectly in the hand.  Not too small and not too big either.  That's why I chose the Pro Max variant before because I took the screen size into consideration.  Now the pro screen size is really ideal in the hand. I've also seen it directly at the official reseller and checked how it feels in the hand.  Is it still comfortable to text or reply to chats with one hand and also how heavy is it?  I have a travel tips YouTube channel where I record all the footage with old iPhones and an iPhone 14 Pro Max which weighs 1/4 kilo plus a thick case.  I have to hold it like this when I want to talk in front of the camera and it makes my hands shake and my little finger becomes concave like this.  I went on a road trip to Flores to the top of Limutu without bringing an action cam.  Finally, when I was riding a motorbike on that trip, I recorded with one hand like this and the other hand holding the motorbike steering wheel. I had to grip it really tightly like this because the wind was also quite strong and my iPhone could fall at any time because it was blown away or hit.  Luckily, it didn't fall.  From there, I thought to myself, hopefully, in the next iPhone release, Apple will give us something really important that we actually want to get the Pro, but because it's not in the Pro series, people end up getting the Pro Max, namely battery life.  Besides the 13 Pro and 16 Pro, the previous Pro variants of the iPhone were definitely wasteful and heated up quickly because of their small bodies, overpowered chipsets, small battery capacities, and bodies that were less able to dissipate heat.  And because before I buy something, I always watch reviews from lots of tag channels to make sure that I won't make the wrong choice. Almost all reviewers say the battery life of the iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max is the best.  compared to previous iPhones.  In addition to Apple giving a larger battery capacity to the series and Pro Max this year, the addition of the Viper Chamber also makes the battery performance of the iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max more durable and the iPhone does not heat up quickly, including the Pro variant too.  Even on The Tech Chap's YouTube channel, the iPhone 17 Pro's battery life is slightly longer than the 16 Pro Max's.  Do you have an idea?  An iPhone that fits perfectly in your hand, has a super powerful chipset, and battery life that can last as long as last year's 16 Pro Max. That's my strong reason for switching from the usual Pro Max because of the screen and battery considerations to the Pro variant.  Apart from that, I rarely edit videos from my iPhone. In fact, I moved the footage to the Mac and then edited there.  But that doesn't mean the 17 Pro is anything special .  Of course, the Pro Max series has more advantages in terms of screen real estate. The 17 Pro Max wins a lot, especially with the brightness setting up to 3000 nits, which is best for scrolling through social media, watching streaming, or playing games on the 17 Pro Max's large screen.  For those of you who like editing photos or videos to post on social media, the large screen of the 17 Pro Max is supported by the greatness of the A19 Pro chipset and 12 GB of RAM, making your video editing experience even smoother. In short, the large screen is really helpful for those of you who do anything on your iPhone, from entertainment, games, editing content to work matters, all of which can be fulfilled.  Yes, it's actually really possible to edit photos or videos using the 17 Pro, but the screen isn't big enough, so our eyes get tired quickly if you edit photos or videos from the 17 Pro's screen for a long time.  The next advantage is definitely the larger battery capacity.  And now it's the iPhone with the longest battery life compared to previously released iPhones.  Many say their iPhone can last all day until tomorrow with high intensity use .  Meanwhile, for normal use, it can last up to 2 days.  I'm sure Promx fans will still choose the Pro Max whatever the reason.  Some people buy it because of the bigger screen and longer-lasting battery, and there are also those who buy the Pro Max variant so people know they can buy a more expensive iPhone. Because it looks bigger physically.  Meanwhile, regarding memory, who knows if the iPhone 17 Pro we bought is 256 GB or 2 TB.  But if you look at the physical size, the difference is clearly visible.  If you really buy Prox to be considered better, then there is no cure for that.  But I'm sure I'm not the only one who chose the Pro Max variant because the previous Pro variants were battery-guzzling and heated up quickly.  We also think that we are buying this cellphone for the long term.  If you just bought it and it's really wasteful and heats up quickly, how will the battery's health be after 1 year and beyond?  Thankfully, Apple has improved battery life this year , especially for the standard iPhone 17 Pro .  In conclusion, all the features on the 17 Pro Max are also on the 17 Pro.  So, whatever you choose is the same.  The difference between the Pro and Pro Max now is only in the screen size and battery life.  I still recommend visiting the nearest reseller shop to check for yourself which one feels most comfortable in your hand.  And what do you guys often use it for? If you're going to spend a lot of screen time watching streaming, playing games, scrolling through social media, editing photos or videos, and don't mind the iPhone's heavier weight and larger body, the iPhone 17 Pro Max is more suitable for you.  But if you want a smaller iPhone size, it's easy to carry in your pocket, easier to reply to chats with one hand, and it's also light in weight for vlogging activities. With a battery life that is now on par with last year's Pro Max series, the iPhone 17 Pro could be the right choice.  Hopefully this information is useful. Thank you for watching and see you in the next video.
